message:
fix(core): re-acquire block device adapter after create_inode to prevent stale reads

When creating a new inode via ext4_create, the block device adapter held
before the create_inode call could still cache the old inode table block.
If the parent inode and the newly allocated inode happen to reside in the
same inode table block, the subsequent ext4_add_dir_entry call would read
the stale (pre-create) version of that block, write the parent's
directory entry into it, and flush it back -- silently overwriting the
freshly created child inode.

The fix re-acquires the block device adapter immediately after
create_inode returns, so that all subsequent reads (in particular the
parent-inode read inside ext4_add_dir_entry) observe the snapshot that
already contains the new inode.  This eliminates the data-loss race
without changing the public API or adding any new locking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
